section.heading: Grants authorized
section.chapeau: The Attorney General may make grants to States, units of local government, territories, nonprofit organizations, and Indian Tribes to—

paragraph.content: develop, implement, and expand comprehensive and clinically-appropriate family-based substance abuse treatment programs as alternatives to incarceration for nonviolent parent drug offenders; and
to 11 So in original. The word “to” probably should not appear. provide prison-based family treatment programs for incarcerated parents of minor children or pregnant women.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 3797s of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Par. (1). Pub. L. 115–391 inserted “nonprofit organizations,” before “and Indian” in introductory provisions.
2016—Par. (2). Pub. L. 114–198 inserted before period at end “or pregnant women”.

note.heading: Construction of 2008 Amendment
note.p: For construction of amendments by Pub. L. 110–199 and requirements for grants made under such amendments, see section 60504 of this title.
